config_files.notes: PUBG uses TslGame as the internal UE4 game name. The file is rewritten on every game exit. Edit only while PUBG is fully closed.
Engine-level overrides. Lower-level than GameUserSettings.ini. Keys added here are not overwritten by in-game settings changes.

keys.effect: Render resolution scale as a percentage of screen resolution. Range 50-100.
keys.notes: PUBG is GPU-heavy in dense loot areas and final circles. Reducing to 80 on 1080p hardware can add 15-25 FPS with minimal visibility impact at long range. NVIDIA DLSS and AMD FSR are available in-game and supersede this key.

PUBG shadow quality is a significant GPU cost driver. Low (0) is the competitive standard — shadows create visual noise that makes spotting enemies harder. Low is both the performance and competitive choice.
Epic textures require 8 GB+ VRAM. On 6 GB cards, set to High (2) to avoid streaming stutter in dense loot buildings.
Low PostProcess is standard for competitive play — disabling heavy depth of field and bloom improves visual clarity when scanning for enemies at distance.

keys.effect: Visual effects (smoke, muzzle flash, explosions) quality: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=epic.
keys.notes: Low effects improves FPS in firefights and reduces GPU spikes from grenades and zone effects. Also reduces smoke grenade visual obstruction size slightly.

Low foliage reduces bush/grass density, which is a significant CPU cost in rural map areas (Erangel fields, Miramar desert edges). Note: Low foliage also reduces the ability to hide in bushes — inform users of this trade-off.

engine_ini_keys.notes: Motion blur in a fast-paced battle royale reduces visibility when panning and causes discomfort for many players. Default off for all profiles.

engine_ini_keys.effect: Depth of field effect quality: 0=off, 1-4=quality levels.
engine_ini_keys.notes: Depth of field blurs non-focused areas. In a visibility-critical game, this is universally disabled by competitive players.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: TAA for quality. Off or FXAA for maximum FPS. TAA adds slight blur to distant foliage which can obscure enemies at range — some competitive players prefer FXAA or off.
manual_only_settings.reason: AA mode is set via in-game menu and stored in GameUserSettings.ini but requires a game restart to apply. Recommend in-game to avoid a mid-file conflict.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on RTX hardware at 1080p+. FSR Quality on AMD. Native Resolution on high-end hardware.
manual_only_settings.reason: PUBG's integrated upscaler selection is in-game only.

notes: PUBG's internal game name in UE4 paths is TslGame (The Survival Lab), not PUBG. The config path is %LOCALAPPDATA%\TslGame\, not %LOCALAPPDATA%\PUBG\.
PUBG uses an anti-cheat system (BATTLEYE). All keys in this profile are config-file based and BattlEye-safe. No binary modifications are recommended.
Low shadow and foliage settings are both a performance optimisation and a competitive advantage in PUBG. Inform users of the gameplay implications before applying competitive presets.
PUBG migrated from UE4 to a heavily modified version in 2022 (PUBG: New Era update). The config key names and paths remain UE4-standard despite renderer improvements.
